page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this server allocates 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It features 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The processor includes 64 KB L1D cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache, with hardware-level support for AES cryptography.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8gd.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier status in both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations. The local NVMe SSD storage and high memory throughput support exceptional database performance, as evidenced by top-tier results in Redis SET operations and Passmark database benchmarks. Additionally, the instance excels in static web serving throughput and multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression.

While multi-core and memory-bound workloads perform exceptionally well, single-core performance is more moderate, placing in the middle 50% for single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests. LLM inference benchmarks show a distinct contrast: text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) models achieves top-tier speeds, whereas text generation for very small (135M) models falls into the bottom 25%.

The c8gd.24xlarge offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads that require fast local scratch space. It is highly suited for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, database hosting, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers a baseline network speed of 40 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database execution, including Redis SET operations. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are moderate, the instance excels in highly parallelized tasks. The combination of high-density ARM compute and fast local NVMe storage makes this instance highly efficient for software compilation, database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.24xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 96 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database and multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.24xlarge features 96 ARM64 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 compute platform featuring dedicated physical cores, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core performance.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for software compilation, Redis caching, and parallel data processing
